NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Contemplation of the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for the water brooks, So pants my soul for You, O God.
NKJV.2: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?
NKJV.3: My tears have been my food day and night, While they continually say to me, “Where is your God?”
NKJV.4: When I remember these things, I pour out my soul within me. For I used to go with the multitude; I went with them to the house of God, With the voice of joy and praise, With a multitude that kept a pilgrim feast.
NKJV.5: Why are you cast down, O my soul? And why are you disquieted within me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise Him For the help of His countenance.
NKJV.6: O my God, my soul is cast down within me; Therefore I will remember You from the land of the Jordan, And from the heights of Hermon, From the Hill Mizar.
NKJV.7: Deep calls unto deep at the noise of Your waterfalls;  All Your waves and billows have gone over me.
NKJV.8: The LORD will command His lovingkindness in the daytime, And in the night His song shall be with me— A prayer to the God of my life.
NKJV.9: I will say to God my Rock,  “Why have You forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?”
NKJV.10: As with a breaking of my bones, My enemies reproach me, While they say to me all day long, “Where is your God?”
NKJV.11: Why are you cast down, O my soul? And why are you disquieted within me? Hope in God; For I shall yet praise Him, The help of my countenance and my God.

ESV.1: As a deer pants for flowing streams, so pants my soul for you, O God.
ESV.2: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?
ESV.3: My tears have been my food day and night, while they say to me all the day long, “Where is your God?”
ESV.4: These things I remember, as I pour out my soul: how I would go with the throng and lead them in procession to the house of God with glad shouts and songs of praise, a multitude keeping festival.
ESV.5: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation
ESV.6: and my God.  My soul is cast down within me; therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan and of Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
ESV.7: Deep calls to deep at the roar of your waterfalls; all your breakers and your waves have gone over me.
ESV.8: By day the LORD commands his steadfast love, and at night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
ESV.9: I say to God, my rock: “Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?”
ESV.10: As with a deadly wound in my bones, my adversaries taunt me, while they say to me all the day long, “Where is your God?”
ESV.11: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why are you in turmoil within me? Hope in God; for I shall again praise him, my salvation and my God.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for the water brooks, So my soul pants for You, O God.
NASB.2: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God; When shall I come and appear before God?
NASB.3: My tears have been my food day and night, While [they] say to me all day long, 'Where is your God?'
NASB.4: These things I remember and I pour out my soul within me. For I used to go along with the throng [and] lead them in procession to the house of God, With the voice of joy and thanksgiving, a multitude keeping festival.
NASB.5: Why are you in despair, O my soul? And [why] have you become disturbed within me? Hope in God, for I shall again praise Him [For] the help of His presence.
NASB.6: O my God, my soul is in despair within me; Therefore I remember You from the land of the Jordan And the peaks of Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
NASB.7: Deep calls to deep at the sound of Your waterfalls; All Your breakers and Your waves have rolled over me.
NASB.8: The LORD will command His lovingkindness in the daytime; And His song will be with me in the night, A prayer to the God of my life.
NASB.9: I will say to God my rock, 'Why have You forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?'
NASB.10: As a shattering of my bones, my adversaries revile me, While they say to me all day long, 'Where is your God?'
NASB.11: Why are you in despair, O my soul? And why have you become disturbed within me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise Him, The help of my countenance and my God.

NLT.1:
As the deer longs for streams of water,
so I long for you, O God.
NLT.2:
I thirst for God, the living God.
When can I go and stand before him?
NLT.3:
Day and night I have only tears for food,
while my enemies continually taunt me, saying,
“Where is this God of yours?”
NLT.4:
My heart is breaking
as I remember how it used to be:
I walked among the crowds of worshipers,
leading a great procession to the house of God,
singing for joy and giving thanks
amid the sound of a great celebration!
NLT.5:
Why am I discouraged?
Why is my heart so sad?
I will put my hope in God!
I will praise him again —
my Savior and
NLT.6:
my God!
Now I am deeply discouraged,
but I will remember you —
even from distant Mount Hermon, the source of the Jordan,
from the land of Mount Mizar.
NLT.7:
I hear the tumult of the raging seas
as your waves and surging tides sweep over me.
NLT.8:
But each day the LORD pours his unfailing love upon me,
and through each night I sing his songs,
praying to God who gives me life.
NLT.9:
“O God my rock,” I cry,
“Why have you forgotten me?
Why must I wander around in grief,
oppressed by my enemies?”
NLT.10:
Their taunts break my bones.
They scoff, “Where is this God of yours?”
NLT.11:
Why am I discouraged?
Why is my heart so sad?
I will put my hope in God!
I will praise him again —
my Savior and my God!

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A contemplation by the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for the water brooks, so my soul pants after you, God.
WEB.2: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?
WEB.3: My tears have been my food day and night, while they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”
WEB.4: These things I remember, and pour out my soul within me, how I used to go with the crowd, and led them to God’s house, with the voice of joy and praise, a multitude keeping a holy day.
WEB.5: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him for the saving help of his presence.
WEB.6: My God, my soul is in despair within me. Therefore I remember you from the land of the Jordan, the heights of Hermon, from the hill Mizar.
WEB.7: Deep calls to deep at the noise of your waterfalls. All your waves and your billows have swept over me.
WEB.8: Yahweh  will command his loving kindness in the daytime. In the night his song shall be with me: a prayer to the God of my life.
WEB.9: I will ask God, my rock, “Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?”
WEB.10: As with a sword in my bones, my adversaries reproach me, while they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”
WEB.11: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him, the saving help of my countenance, and my God.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A of the sons of Korah. [1] As a deer longs for flowing streams,
so I long for you, God. ⓝ
CSB17.2:
I thirst for God, the living God.
When can I come and appear before God? ⓐ
CSB17.3:
My tears have been my food day and night,
while all day long people say to me,
“Where is your God?” ⓑ
CSB17.4:
I remember this as I pour out my heart: ⓒ
how I walked with many,
leading the festive procession to the house of God,
with joyful and thankful shouts. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
Why, my soul, are you so dejected?
Why are you in such turmoil?
Put your hope in God, for I will still praise him,
my Savior and my God. ⓔ
CSB17.6:
I [1] am deeply depressed;
therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan
and the peaks of Hermon, from Mount Mizar. ⓕ
CSB17.7:
Deep calls to deep in the roar of your waterfalls;
all your breakers and your billows have swept over me. ⓖ
CSB17.8:
The LORD will send his faithful love by day; ⓗ
his song will be with me in the night —
a prayer to the God of my life.
CSB17.9:
I will say to God, my rock, ⓘ
“Why have you forgotten me?
Why must I go about in sorrow
because of the enemy’s oppression?” ⓙ
CSB17.10:
My adversaries taunt me,
as if crushing my bones,
while all day long they say to me,
“Where is your God?” ⓚ
CSB17.11:
Why, my soul, are you so dejected?
Why are you in such turmoil?
Put your hope in God, for I will still praise him,
my Savior and my God. ⓛ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of the sons of Korah. As the deer pants [longingly] for the water brooks,
So my soul pants [longingly] for You, O God.
AMP.2:
My soul (my life, my inner self) thirsts for God, for the living God.
When will I come and see the face of God?
AMP.3:
My tears have been my food day and night,
While they say to me all day long, "Where is your God?"
AMP.4:
These things I [vividly] remember as I pour out my soul;
How I used to go along before the great crowd of people and lead them in procession to the house of God [like a choirmaster before his singers, timing the steps to the music and the chant of the song],
With the voice of joy and thanksgiving, a great crowd keeping a festival.
AMP.5:
Why are you in despair, O my soul?
And why have you become restless and disturbed within me?
Hope in God and wait expectantly for Him, for I shall again praise Him
For the help of His presence.
AMP.6:
O my God, my soul is in despair within me [the burden more than I can bear];
Therefore I will [fervently] remember You from the land of the Jordan
And the peaks of [Mount] Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
AMP.7:
Deep calls to deep at the [thundering] sound of Your waterfalls;
All Your breakers and Your waves have rolled over me.
AMP.8:
Yet the LORD will command His lovingkindness in the daytime,
And in the night His song will be with me,
A prayer to the God of my life.
AMP.9:
I will say to God my rock, "Why have You forgotten me?
Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?"
AMP.10:
As a crushing of my bones [with a sword], my adversaries taunt me,
While they say continually to me, "Where is your God?"
AMP.11:
Why are you in despair, O my soul?
Why have you become restless and disquieted within me?
Hope in God and wait expectantly for Him, for I shall yet praise Him,
The help of my countenance and my God.

MSG.1:
A psalm of the sons of Korah
A white-tailed deer drinks
from the creek;
I want to drink God,
deep draughts of God.
MSG.2:
I'm thirsty for God-alive.
I wonder, "Will I ever make it—
arrive and drink in God's presence?"
MSG.3:
I'm on a diet of tears—
tears for breakfast, tears for supper.
All day long
people knock at my door,
Pestering,
"Where is this God of yours?"
MSG.4:
These are the things I go over and over,
emptying out the pockets of my life.
I was always at the head of the worshiping crowd,
right out in front,
Leading them all,
eager to arrive and worship,
Shouting praises, singing thanksgiving—
celebrating, all of us, God's feast!
MSG.5:
Why are you down in the dumps, dear soul?
Why are you crying the blues?
Fix my eyes on God—
soon I'll be praising again.
He puts a smile on my face.
He's my God.
MSG.6:
When my soul is in the dumps, I rehearse
everything I know of you,
From Jordan depths to Hermon heights,
including Mount Mizar.
MSG.7:
Chaos calls to chaos,
to the tune of whitewater rapids.
Your breaking surf, your thundering breakers
crash and crush me.
MSG.8:
Then God promises to love me all day,
sing songs all through the night!
My life is God's prayer.
MSG.9:
Sometimes I ask God, my rock-solid God,
"Why did you let me down?
Why am I walking around in tears,
harassed by enemies?"
MSG.10:
They're out for the kill, these
tormentors with their obscenities,
Taunting day after day,
"Where is this God of yours?"
MSG.11:
Why are you down in the dumps, dear soul?
Why are you crying the blues?
Fix my eyes on God—
soon I'll be praising again.
He puts a smile on my face.
He's my God.

NIV.1:
BOOK II
Psalms 42–72
Psalm 42
For the director of music. A maskil of the Sons of Korah.
As the deer pants for streams of water,
so my soul pants for you, O God.
NIV.2:
My soul thirsts for God, for the living God.
When can I go and meet with God?
NIV.3:
My tears have been my food
day and night,
while men say to me all day long,
“Where is your God?”
NIV.4:
These things I remember
as I pour out my soul:
how I used to go with the multitude,
leading the procession to the house of God,
with shouts of joy and thanksgiving
among the festive throng.
NIV.5:
Why are you downcast, O my soul?
Why so disturbed within me?
Put your hope in God,
for I will yet praise him,
my Savior and
NIV.6:
my God.
My soul is downcast within me;
therefore I will remember you
from the land of the Jordan,
the heights of Hermon—from Mount Mizar.
NIV.7:
Deep calls to deep
in the roar of your waterfalls;
all your waves and breakers
have swept over me.
NIV.8:
By day the Lord directs his love,
at night his song is with me—
a prayer to the God of my life.
NIV.9:
I say to God my Rock,
“Why have you forgotten me?
Why must I go about mourning,
oppressed by the enemy?”
NIV.10:
My bones suffer mortal agony
as my foes taunt me,
saying to me all day long,
“Where is your God?”
NIV.11:
Why are you downcast, O my soul?
Why so disturbed within me?
Put your hope in God,
for I will yet praise him,
my Savior and my God.

the face of God?

day and night,
while people say to me continually,

NRSVCE.4:
These things I remember,
as I pour out my soul:
how I went with the throng,
and led them in procession to the house of God,
with glad shouts and songs of thanksgiving,
a multitude keeping festival.
NRSVCE.5:
Why are you cast down, O my soul,
and why are you disquieted within me?
Hope in God; for I shall again praise him,
my help
NRSVCE.6:
and my God.
My soul is cast down within me;
therefore I remember you
from the land of Jordan and of Hermon,
from Mount Mizar.
NRSVCE.7:
Deep calls to deep
at the thunder of your cataracts;

NRSVCE.8:
By day the LORD commands his steadfast love,

NRSVCE.10:

my help and my God.

NET.1: As a deer longs for streams of water, so I long for you, O God!
NET.2: I thirst for God, for the living God. I say, “When will I be able to go and appear in God’s presence?”
NET.3: I cannot eat, I weep day and night; all day long they say to me, “Where is your God?”
NET.4: I will remember and weep! For I was once walking along with the great throng to the temple of God, shouting and giving thanks along with the crowd as we celebrated the holy festival.
NET.5: Why are you depressed, O my soul? Why are you upset? Wait for God! For I will again give thanks to my God for his saving intervention.
NET.6: I am depressed, so I will pray to you while I am trapped here in the region of the upper Jordan, from Hermon, from Mount Mizar.
NET.7: One deep stream calls out to another at the sound of your waterfalls; all your billows and waves overwhelm me.
NET.8: By day the LORD decrees his loyal love, and by night he gives me a song, a prayer to the living God.
NET.9: I will pray to God, my high ridge: “Why do you ignore me? Why must I walk around mourning because my enemies oppress me?”
NET.10: My enemies’ taunts cut into me to the bone, as they say to me all day long, “Where is your God?”
NET.11: Why are you depressed, O my soul? Why are you upset? Wait for God! For I will again give thanks to my God for his saving intervention.
